name: HODGKIN DISEASE
definition: HODGKIN LYMPHOMA disease of T cells 1÷1 rarely affecting GI tract → mostly 2ndary involvement by tumor extension from adjacent

demographics.Frequency: 0.75% of all cancers diagnosed each year; 40% of all lymphomas
demographics.Age: bimodal peaks at age 25-30 years and 75-80 years; very rare in children < 5 years; M÷F
demographics.Histo: Hodgkin and Reed-Sternberg cells = binucleate cells with prominent centrally located
demographics.Location: dorsolumbar spine > pelvis > ribs > femora > sternum

dominant_finding: concentric thickening of affected bowel wall
distribution: dorsolumbar spine > pelvis > ribs > femora > sternum
discriminating_features: coarse reticulonodular pattern contiguous with mediastinum = direct extension

general: coarse reticulonodular pattern contiguous with mediastinum = direct extension
homogeneous confluent infiltrates with shaggy borders
multiple nodules < 1 cm in diameter (DDx: metastatic disease)
CXR: on initial film adenopathy identified in 50%
necrotic lymph nodes (commonly nodular sclerosing type)
lymph nodes may calcify following radiation / chemotherapy
consolidation with bulging borders: necrotizing bacterial pneumonia
rapidly developing cavitation within consolidation: anaerobes / fungus
NUC: usually wide ill-defined lesion edge / sclerotic margin
predominantly osteolytic with blurred borders; rarely sclerotic / mixed lytic-
vertebral osteolysis with collapse / patchy sclerosis / "ivory vertebra" / mixed lytic +
gouge defect of anterior vertebral body margin ← erosion by lymph nodes
osteolysis of sternum ← proximity to thoracic lymph ducts
MR: hypo- / isointense nodules on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI
reduced enhancement compared with normal spleen
CT: concentric thickening of affected bowel wall
narrow rigid obstructive lesion (DDx: scirrhous ca.)
wall thickening + smoothly lobulated outer border
abundance of desmoplastic reaction (DDx from NHL)
CNS (uncommon): white matter mass, typically periventricular / basal ganglionic / cerebellar
epidural mass with spinal cord compression (in 3.0-7.6%) from extension of
THYMUS (30-56%): remains enlarged after treatment in 33% ← recurrent disease / rebound hyperplasia /
CHEST WALL (6.4%): infiltration of parasternal soft tissues by direct extension from internal mammary nodes
LIVER (6-20%): discrete nodules (10%) = miliary lesions of < 10 mm
diffuse disease (87%) = patchy irregular infiltrates in portal areas
SPLEEN: diffuse involvement (not detectable by imaging) ± splenomegaly
hypoechoic hypoattenuating nodules with reduced contrast enhancement
GI TRACT (10-15%): diffuse fold thickening / ulcerated mass in stomach
irregular luminal narrowing + nodularity in esophagus, small bowel, colon
GU TRACT (extremely rare): perirenal / renal masses ← invasion from surrounding nodes

clinical.Cx: focal stenosis, obstruction ← greater degree of fibrosis than lymphoma
